name: loop_slice_gap
description: Loop Slice 'Gap' option (task 0253, depends on Split/0251 + Cap Sections/0252). Reuses the 0251/0252 unit cube (8 verts / 12 edges / 6 quad faces); the seed edge is the vertical edge (1,-1,-1)-(1,1,-1), so the ring cuts a horizontal belt around the 4 side faces at y=0 (4 vertical rails). Split (always ON here) duplicates each rail midpoint into a coincident lo/hi pair; Cap Sections (ON) bridges each lo cut edge to its hi twin with a cap quad — a ring of 4 zero-area cap quads while lo/hi coincide (28 edges / 14 faces, one closed manifold). Gap opens a width between the two boundary loops by pushing each seam pair apart along its rail (cut) direction, symmetric about the split line: gap G moves each vert G/2 toward the corner on ITS OWN side of the loop. With G=0.4 the 4 rails are vertical (±y), so the top-shell loop verts move to y=+0.2 and the bottom-shell loop verts to y=-0.2 (or vice versa — symmetric, so the vertex SET is the same either way). The 8 loop verts, coincident at 4 midpoints while G=0, now occupy 8 distinct positions {[±1,±0.2,±1]} — proof the two loops separated by exactly G and the cap quads gained real (non-zero) area. Topology is UNCHANGED from the caps-on case (16 verts / 28 edges / 14 faces); Gap only relocates the duplicated verts. The gap_zero case pins the byte-for-byte invariant: G=0 leaves every seam pair coincident, identical to 0251/0252. Reference-independent geometry law: each seam pair separates by exactly G along its pre-op rail direction; nothing else moves.
source: Analytic self-golden. The 'Gap' rule is DERIVED from the live-captured reference `gap` attribute (toolcards/mesh.loopSlice spec.json, task 0244 live probe: type=distance, 'Gap between split parts -- with Split on, defines a width around the split line, thickening the cut'; the live 54.2 mm reading is a sticky seeded pref, NOT a fresh factory default, so vibe3d's factory default is 0 = coincident). The reference loop-slice gesture cannot be driven headlessly (needs a human VNC drive), so the ON/OFF gap geometry is NOT independently re-captured live. Implementation choice (documented): the gap is opened SYMMETRICALLY about the split line (each of the two coincident verts moves gap/2, matching 'a width AROUND the split line') along the rail edge va->vb, which lies on the surface and runs perpendicular to the inserted loop — the direction the two sides pull apart. Each seam vert is pushed toward the corner on its own side of the loop, so a whole shell moves consistently even though the canonical va corner varies per rail. Flagged honestly.
